composes.brief_description: The fundamental transfer object for SQL operations.
The unique identifier for the base Transfer object.
composes.name: id

composes.detailed_description: The Base Transfer Object simply contains an identifier, used for SQL operations and assumed to be the primary key for the associated table. Any subsequent transfer object should inherit from this to enforce the primary key behavior.
composes.name: BaseTransferObject

composes.brief_description: Get or create a DAO for the specified type.
composes.name: getDAO

composes.brief_description: Flush all buffered records from all registered DAOs.
composes.detailed_description: Iterates over all DataAccessObject instances created via getDAO<T>() and calls insert() on each to flush their write buffers to the database. Thread-safe: Can be called while other threads add records via addToBuffer(). This is a convenience method equivalent to calling dao.insert() on each DAO individually. The flush order is deterministic (creation order of DAOs).
composes.name: flushAllDAOs

composes.name: getRawDB

composes.brief_description: Check if a transaction is currently active.
composes.detailed_description: true if a transaction is active (autocommit is disabled)
composes.name: isInTransaction

composes.brief_description: Execute a function within a transaction.
composes.detailed_description: This is a convenience method that wraps a function in a transaction. If the function throws an exception, the transaction is rolled back. If the function completes successfully, the transaction is committed. func The function to execute within the transaction TransactionError if transaction operations fail Any exception thrown by the function (after rollback) Usage: db.withTransaction([&]() { auto & dao = db.getDAO<MyObject>(); dao.insert(obj1); dao.insert(obj2); });
composes.name: withTransaction

composes.brief_description: Resolve the foreign key to the full object.
composes.definition: std::optional< std::reference_wrapper< const T > > cpp_sqlite::ForeignKey< T >::resolve
composes.detailed_description: db Reference to the database Optional containing the loaded object, or empty if not found
composes.name: resolve

composes.brief_description: Check if this FK is set (non-zero ID).
composes.name: isSet

composes.brief_description: The ID of the referenced object.
composes.name: id

composes.detailed_description: This allows lazy loading - the full object is not loaded from the database until explicitly requested via the resolve() method. Use this when you want to reference another table without the overhead of loading the full nested object during SELECT operations. Example: struct Vertex3D : public cpp_sqlite::BaseTransferObject { float x, y, z; }; struct RigidBody : public cpp_sqlite::BaseTransferObject { std::string name; ForeignKey<Vertex3D> centerOfMass; // Lazy - just stores ID Vertex3D position; // Eager - auto-loads full object }; // Usage: auto body = bodyDAO.selectById(1); std::cout << "Center FK ID: " << body->centerOfMass.id << "\n" ; // Load the full vertex only when needed auto vertex = body->centerOfMass.resolve(db); if (vertex) { std::cout << "Center: " << vertex->x << ", " << vertex->y << "\n" ; }
composes.name: ForeignKey
configure
composes.qualified_name: cpp_sqlite::Logger::configure(const std::string &loggerName="cpp_sqlite", const std::string &logFile="cpp_sqlite.log", spdlog::level::level_enum level=spdlog::level::info)

composes.brief_description: Container for repeated (one-to-many) fields in transfer objects.

composes.detailed_description: < The underlying vector of transfer objects Member must be named 'data' for compatibility with boost::describe
composes.name: data

composes.detailed_description: This represents a collection of transfer objects that forms a one-to-many relationship in the database (typically requiring a junction table). T Must be a SingleTransferObject (not another RepeatedField) Example usage: struct Tag : BaseTransferObject { std::string name; }; struct Article : BaseTransferObject { std::string title; RepeatedFieldTransferObject<Tag> tags; // One-to-many relationship }; The constraint SingleTransferObject ensures you cannot nest repeated fields: RepeatedFieldTransferObject<RepeatedFieldTransferObject<Tag>> // Compile error!
composes.name: RepeatedFieldTransferObject

composes.brief_description: RAII-style transaction management for SQLite database operations.
Begin a new transaction.
composes.detailed_description: db Reference to the database TransactionError if BEGIN fails If a transaction is already active, this creates a savepoint instead.
composes.name: Transaction

composes.brief_description: Destructor - automatically rolls back if not committed.
composes.name: ~Transaction

composes.brief_description: Commit the transaction.
composes.detailed_description: TransactionError if COMMIT fails After calling commit() , the destructor will not rollback. For savepoints, this releases the savepoint.
composes.name: commit

composes.brief_description: Explicitly rollback the transaction.
composes.detailed_description: TransactionError if ROLLBACK fails After calling rollback() , the destructor will not attempt another rollback. For savepoints, this rolls back to the savepoint.
composes.name: rollback

composes.brief_description: Check if the transaction is still active (not committed or rolled back).
composes.detailed_description: true if the transaction is active
composes.name: isActive

composes.brief_description: Check if this is a savepoint (nested transaction).
composes.detailed_description: true if this is a savepoint
composes.name: isSavepoint

composes.brief_description: Get the savepoint name (empty if not a savepoint).
composes.detailed_description: The savepoint name
composes.name: getSavepointName

composes.brief_description: Execute a SQL statement and handle errors.
composes.name: executeSQL

composes.brief_description: Reference to the database.
composes.name: db_

composes.brief_description: Whether this transaction is still active.
composes.name: isActive_

composes.brief_description: Whether this is a savepoint (nested transaction).
composes.name: isSavepoint_

composes.brief_description: The savepoint name (if this is a savepoint).
composes.name: savepointName_

composes.brief_description: Static counter for generating unique savepoint names.
composes.name: savepointCounter_

composes.detailed_description: This class provides automatic transaction management with the following features: Automatic rollback if the transaction is not explicitly committed Support for nested transactions via savepoints Exception-safe resource management Usage: Database db( "test.db" , true ); { Transaction txn(db); // ... perform database operations ... txn.commit(); // Explicit commit } // If commit() is not called, destructor will rollback For nested transactions: Database db( "test.db" , true ); { Transaction outer(db); // ... some operations ... { Transaction inner(db); // Creates a savepoint // ... more operations ... inner.commit(); // Releases savepoint } outer.commit(); }
composes.name: Transaction

composes.brief_description: Exception thrown when a transaction operation fails.
composes.name: TransactionError

composes.name: isInitialized

composes.brief_description: Perform insert operation with buffered data.
composes.name: insert

composes.brief_description: Clear the internal data buffer.
composes.name: clearBuffer

detailed_description: Abstract base class for all Data Access Objects Provides common interface for polymorphic storage
name: DAOBase

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::CopyableTransferObject = TransferObject<T> && std::copyable<T>
name: CopyableTransferObject

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::isBlob = std::is_same_v<T, std::vector<uint8_t>>
name: isBlob

initializer: template<typename C> concept cpp_sqlite::IsRepeatedFieldTransferObject = requires(C c) { // 1. Check for a member named `data` { c.data }; // 2. Check that the member `data` is a std::vector requires kIsVectorV <decltype(c.data)>; // 3. Check the element type of the vector against the HasToString concept requires ValidTransferObject<typename decltype(c.data)::value_type>; }
name: IsRepeatedFieldTransferObject

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::isString = std::is_same_v<T, std::string>
name: isString

detailed_description: A type supported by the database is either: A basic integral type A floating point type A string A BLOB (binary data as std::vector<uint8_t>) A single transfer object Or a repeated field of transfer objects
initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::isSupportedDBType = isIntegral<T> || floatingPoint<T> || isString<T> || isBlob<T> || ValidTransferObject<T>
name: isSupportedDBType

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::MovableTransferObject = TransferObject<T> && std::movable<T>
name: MovableTransferObject

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::TransferObject = std::derived_from<T, BaseTransferObject >
name: TransferObject

initializer: template<typename T> concept cpp_sqlite::ValidTransferObject = TransferObject<T> && DefaultConstructibleTransferObject<T>
name: ValidTransferObject

implementation:
*        whether this is a read-only database.
   */
  Database(std::string url,
           bool allowWrite,

/*!
   * \brief Get or create a DAO for the specified type
   */
  template <ValidTransferObject T>

* features:
 * - Automatic rollback if the transaction is not explicitly committed
 * - Support for nested transactions via savepoints
 * - Exception-safe resource management
 *
 * Usage:
 * \code
 *   Database db("test.db", true);
 *   {
 *     Transaction txn(db);
 *     // ... perform database operations ...
 *     txn.commit();  // Explicit commit
 *   }
 *   // If commit() is not called, destructor will rollback
